free_text: Reexamination of cross sections of the
Target foils were 10 x 10 mm rectangular Mo foils with
natural isotopic composition from Goodfellow, UK
measured average thickness 11.98 um, chemical purity:
Activation method was used. Irradiation were
done at 16 and 36.4 MeV proton energies.
The beam was extracted from the cyclotron and
the irradiation was done in a Faraday-type target
Energy degradation in each layers the stack
was calculated by using the semi-empirical polynomial
The beam energy and intensity were monitored
Uncertainty on the energy scale includes
cumulative effects of initial beam energy, stopping
estimation influenced by target thickness and beam
straggling. Throughout the stack it increases from
0.25MeV for the first foil to 0.45MeV for 16MeV proton
irradiation and 1.56 MeV for the 36MeV irradiation.
The uncertainty on the cross section values
are estimated in the standard way as the square root
of the quadratic summation of the independently
parameters is not included. Overall uncertainty were
The experimental data were compared to the
results of the theoretical model codes TENDL-2013.
The measured experimental data were compared to
So NDS, Major revision in 002 and 004
